e_visa.nationality: All nationalities requiring a visa (not on exemption list)
e_visa.notes: South Africa operates an eVisa system via the DHA ePermit portal (ehome.dha.gov.za). Eligible applicants must land at OR Tambo, Cape Town, or Lanseria International Airports. eVisa replaces traditional paper visa; validity and stay periods vary by nationality and visa type. Visa-exempt travelers may also apply for an ETA for faster border processing.

eta.nationality: Eligible visa-required travelers (initial phases: China, India, Indonesia, Mexico); expanding to all visa-required nationalities
eta.notes: South Africa's ETA (Electronic Travel Authorisation) launched in September/October 2025, initially for G20 participants and then extended to general tourists from China, India, Indonesia, and Mexico. As of April 2026, the ETA is reported as 'fully rolled out.' The ETA allows travelers from eligible visa-required countries to visit for tourism/visitor purposes without a traditional paper visa. Visa-exempt travelers are not required to use the ETA but may apply optionally. Applications processed within 24 hours per Minister Schreiber. Initial entry points: OR Tambo (Johannesburg) and Cape Town International Airports, expanding to secondary airports. Work is not permitted on an ETA. Validity: up to 90 days, extendable once for an additional 90 days (max 180 days total).

visa_policy.notes: Visa exemption list sourced from official DIRCO (Department of International Relations and Cooperation) Paris Embassy PDF dated November 2024, DIRCO UK High Commission page updated March 2024, DIRCO Canberra High Commission page, DIRCO Sweden Embassy notice September 2025 (Estonia/Latvia), and DHA official exempt-countries page (issued 9 December 2025). South Africa has no visa-on-arrival policy; travelers either have visa exemption, apply for an eVisa (online), or apply for an ETA. The ETA system launched September/October 2025. Nine East European countries were added to the ordinary passport visa-free list via DHA Government Notices in August–October 2025, all confirmed on the DHA exempt-countries page (December 2025): Hungary (updated from 30-day to 90-day), Slovenia, Romania, Latvia, Estonia, Croatia (all from 27 Aug 2025), and Bulgaria, Lithuania, Slovakia (from 24 Oct 2025). Discrepancies remain between sources for Israel, Italy, Jamaica, Japan, and Russian Federation — present in DIRCO UK and Canberra pages but absent from the DIRCO Paris PDF; classified as visa_free based on two official DIRCO mission pages. Pakistan and Bangladesh ordinary passport holders require a transit visa even if transiting (effective 1 August 2022). ZEP (Zimbabwe Exemption Permits) extended to 28 May 2027; LEP (Lesotho Exemption Permits) extended to 28 May 2027 per Minister's Immigration Directives Nos. 20 and 21 of 2025 (7 October 2025). March 2026 DHA document relates to processing-delay temporary concessions only, not new visa exemptions.

visa_types.name: Visitor's Visa (Tourist) - Up to 90 days
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign nationals intending to visit South Africa temporarily for tourism, family or friend visits, conferences, or sports events.
visa_types.notes: Visa-exempt nationalities do not need this visa for stays up to their permitted period. Non-exempt travelers may apply via eVisa (ehome.dha.gov.za) or ETA (eta.dha.gov.za). Visitors may not work on this visa. Fees updated annually and vary by nationality/currency.

visa_types.name: Visitor's Visa (Tourist) - More than 90 days
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign nationals intending to stay in South Africa for tourism or short-term non-work purposes beyond 90 days.
visa_types.notes: Application submitted at South African embassy/consulate abroad or via ePermit portal. Specific duration determined on a case-by-case basis.

visa_types.name: Visitor's Visa (Business) - Short-term
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreigners visiting South Africa for business purposes such as meetings, negotiations, or conferences for up to 90 days.
visa_types.notes: Short-term business visits; holder may not be employed by a South African entity. Separate from the long-term Business Visa (investment/establishment).

visa_types.name: Business Visa
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign nationals who intend to establish or invest in a business in South Africa as contemplated in Section 15 of the Immigration Act.
visa_types.notes: Requires minimum capital investment as determined by the Minister; applicant's business must employ at least 60% South African citizens or permanent residents. Renewable. Governed by Section 15 of the Immigration Act 2002.

visa_types.name: General Work Visa
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign nationals who have a confirmed offer of employment in South Africa in an occupation where South African citizens or permanent residents are not available.
visa_types.notes: Valid for up to 5 years. Employer must demonstrate that no suitable South African candidate is available (labour market test). Governed by Section 19(2) of the Immigration Act 2002.

visa_types.name: Critical Skills Work Visa
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign nationals whose occupation appears on the South African Critical Skills List, allowing them to work for a South African employer.
visa_types.notes: Valid for up to 5 years. Requires confirmation from a professional body/council recognised by SAQA. Employment offer required. Critical Skills List is gazetted periodically. Governed by Section 19(4) of the Immigration Act. Requirements version effective 9 October 2024.

visa_types.name: Intra-Company Transfer Work Visa
visa_types.purpose: Issued to employees of multinational corporations being transferred to a South African branch, subsidiary, or affiliate within the same group.
visa_types.notes: Valid for up to 4 years. Non-renewable and non-extendable. Governed by Section 19(7) of the Immigration Act 2002.

visa_types.name: Corporate Visa
visa_types.purpose: Enables corporations to employ a predetermined number of foreign nationals in specific occupations where South African workers are unavailable.
visa_types.notes: Valid for up to 3 years. Issued to the corporation; employees named under the corporate visa are then authorised to work. Governed by Section 21 of the Immigration Act 2002.

visa_types.name: Section 11(2) Short-term Work Authorisation
visa_types.purpose: Permits foreign nationals to perform short-term work in South Africa as a sub-category of the Visitor's Visa framework.
visa_types.notes: Governed by Section 11(2) of the Immigration Act 2002. For short-term assignments, filming, performance, etc. not exceeding 90 days.

visa_types.name: Remote Work Visa (Port of Entry Visa)
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign nationals employed by a foreign-based employer who wish to work remotely from South Africa for more than 90 days.
visa_types.notes: Minimum gross annual salary of R650,796.00 required. Applicant must hold a valid employment contract signed by both parties. Must be resident in a country with valid long-term residence status. B1/B2 US visa holders must apply from their country of origin. Requires FBI background check, medical report, and proof of accommodation. SARS registration required if present for more than 183 aggregate days. Fee listed is USD $36 from US applications. Added to Immigration Regulations effective 28 March 2024.

visa_types.name: Study Visa
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign nationals intending to undertake full-time study at an accredited educational institution in South Africa.
visa_types.notes: Valid for the duration of the course. Holder may work up to 20 hours per week. Must have medical cover with a registered South African medical scheme. Governed by Section 18 of the Immigration Act 2002.

visa_types.name: Exchange Visa
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign nationals under 25 years of age participating in cultural, economic, or social exchange programmes in South Africa.
visa_types.notes: Valid for up to 12 months. Non-renewable and non-extendable. Applicant must be under 25 years of age. Governed by Section 18 of the Immigration Act 2002.

visa_types.name: Retired Person's Visa
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign nationals who wish to retire in South Africa on a temporary basis for limited or seasonal periods.
visa_types.notes: Requires proof of minimum monthly income of R37,000 or minimum net worth of R37,000 (note: official website states R37,000; actual threshold may be higher - verify current gazette). Holder may not work. Governed by Section 17 of the Immigration Act 2002.

visa_types.name: Transit Visa
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign nationals passing through South Africa en route to a third country, particularly those intending to obtain long-term residence in a neighboring country.
visa_types.notes: Required for certain nationalities transiting through South Africa even without leaving the international transit area. Pakistan and Bangladesh ordinary passport holders require a transit visa effective 1 August 2022. Governed by Section 11(1) of the Immigration Act 2002.

visa_types.name: Medical Treatment Visa
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign nationals who need to travel to South Africa for life-saving or specialised medical treatment unavailable in their home country.
visa_types.notes: Valid for up to 6 months. Requires letter from a South African medical practitioner confirming treatment. Holder may not work. Governed by Section 11(4) of the Immigration Act 2002.

visa_types.name: Relative's Visa
visa_types.purpose: Issued to relatives of South African citizens or permanent residents wishing to live in South Africa for an extended period.
visa_types.notes: Valid for up to 2 years. The South African relative must provide a financial assurance of at least R8,500 per month per person. Governed by Section 18 of the Immigration Act 2002. Spouses of South African citizens/permanent residents may apply under Section 11(6) Spousal Visa.

visa_types.name: Spousal / Life Partner Visa - Section 11(6)
visa_types.purpose: Issued to the spouse or life partner of a South African citizen or permanent resident who intends to visit or reside in South Africa.
visa_types.notes: Governed by Section 11(6) of the Immigration Act 2002. Allows the holder to work in South Africa. Must demonstrate a bona fide spousal or life partnership relationship.

visa_types.name: Diplomatic Visa
visa_types.purpose: Issued to accredited diplomatic personnel and their dependents assigned to South Africa in an official diplomatic capacity.
visa_types.notes: Issued to diplomats accrediting to South Africa. Diplomatic passport holders from certain countries still require a port-of-entry accreditation visa (e.g., USA, Australia, Germany). Managed via DIRCO (Department of International Relations and Cooperation).

visa_types.name: Treaty Visa
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign nationals conducting activities in South Africa under the terms of an international treaty or agreement to which South Africa is a party.
visa_types.notes: Governed by international agreements to which South Africa is a signatory. Specific terms depend on the relevant treaty.

visa_types.name: Electronic Travel Authorisation (ETA)
visa_types.purpose: An online pre-travel authorisation for eligible visa-required nationalities visiting South Africa for tourism or short-term purposes, replacing the traditional paper visa for qualifying travellers.
visa_types.notes: Launched September/October 2025. Initial eligible nationalities: China, India, Indonesia, Mexico; expanding to all visa-required nationalities. Stay extendable once for additional 90 days (max 180 days total). Entry initially limited to OR Tambo and Cape Town International Airports, expanding to secondary airports. Work not permitted. Processing within 24 hours per Minister Schreiber. Not required for visa-exempt travellers.

visa_types.name: eVisa (ePermit)
visa_types.purpose: An online visa application system allowing visa-required foreign nationals to apply for South African visitor, study, business, and other visa categories digitally.
visa_types.notes: Available at ehome.dha.gov.za. Eligible applicants must enter through OR Tambo, Cape Town, or Lanseria International Airports. The eVisa is a digital form of the paper visa covering all visa categories available under the Immigration Act. Replaces traditional embassy paper applications for eligible nationalities.

visa_types.name: Permanent Residence Permit
visa_types.purpose: Grants foreign nationals the right to reside permanently in South Africa without time limitations, available under various categories including retirement, business, critical skills, family, and financial independence.
visa_types.notes: Governed by Section 27 of the Immigration Act 2002. Categories: (a) spouse/life partner of SA citizen, (b) critical skills, (c) business/investor, (d) refugee, (e) child of SA citizen, (f) financially independent. Applicants typically must hold a temporary residence visa for 5 years before qualifying (except certain categories). Does not automatically confer citizenship; naturalisation requires additional years of PR.

cbi.notes: South Africa does not offer a Citizenship-by-Investment (CBI) program. No official government source was found advertising such a program. The South African Citizenship Act 1995 provides for citizenship by birth, descent, and naturalisation only. Naturalisation requires lawful admission, permanent residence, and extended ordinary residence.

programs.name: Permanent Residence - Business / Investor Category
programs.notes: Section 27(c) of the Immigration Act 2002: Permanent residence for foreigners who intend to establish or have a business in South Africa. Specific minimum investment amounts are not stated on the official DHA page scraped; applicants must meet criteria per Regulation 24(4). No golden visa or standalone investor residence program with a specific minimum investment threshold was found on official sources.

programs.name: Permanent Residence - Financial Independence Category
programs.notes: Section 27(f) of the Immigration Act 2002: Permanent residence for foreigners who are financially independent. Specific minimum net worth/income thresholds not stated on the official DHA page scraped.

programs.name: Permanent Residence - Critical Skills Category
programs.notes: Section 27(b) of the Immigration Act 2002: Permanent residence for foreigners with exceptional/critical skills and qualifications. Applicants must be on the current Critical Skills List. Work visa holders may apply for direct permanent residence after 5 years.

programs.name: Critical Skills Work Visa
programs.eligibility: Foreign nationals whose occupation appears on the South African Critical Skills List; must provide confirmation from a relevant professional body/council/board recognised by SAQA; valid employment offer required; visa issued for up to 5 years
programs.notes: Governed by Section 19(4) of the Immigration Act. Critical Skills List is updated periodically and published in the Government Gazette. Effective requirements version: 9 October 2024.

programs.name: Remote Work Visa (Digital Nomad Visa)
programs.eligibility: Foreign nationals employed by a foreign-based employer; must hold a valid employment contract signed by both applicant and employer; minimum gross salary of R650,796.00 per annum; must be resident in a country with a valid long-term residence status (e.g., Green Card, work visa); B1/B2 US visa holders not eligible (must apply in country of origin)
programs.notes: Port of Entry Visa (Remote Work Visa) exceeding 90 days. Official requirements document published by the South African Embassy (Washington DC). Minimum salary threshold: R650,796.00 per annum. Added to Immigration Regulations effective 28 March 2024. Application fee: USD $36.00 (from the US). Requires FBI criminal background check, medical report, proof of accommodation, and South African Revenue Service registration if present for more than 183 aggregate days.

validation_notes: CORRECTED: Hungary ordinary passport entry updated from 30 days to 90 days per calendar year, effective 27 August 2025 per DHA Government Notice.
ADDED: Estonia (EST) - new visa_free entry for ordinary passports, 90 days/calendar year, effective 27 August 2025. Sourced from DIRCO Sweden official PDF (September 2025).
ADDED: Latvia (LVA) - new visa_free entry for ordinary passports, 90 days/calendar year, effective 27 August 2025. Sourced from DIRCO Sweden official PDF (September 2025).
ADDED: Slovenia (SVN) - new visa_free entry for ordinary passports, 90 days/calendar year, effective 27 August 2025. Sourced from SATSA (citing DHA Government Notice).

ADDED: Croatia (HRV) - new visa_free entry for ordinary passports, 90 days/calendar year, effective 27 August 2025. Diplomatic/service passport exemption retained in conditional_access. Sourced from SATSA (citing DHA Government Notice).

ADDED: Romania (ROU) - new visa_free entry for ordinary passports, 90 days/calendar year, effective 27 August 2025. Was missing from visa_free entirely (diplomatic-only entry existed in conditional_access). Sourced from SATSA (citing DHA Government Notice).

ADDED: Bulgaria (BGR) - new visa_free entry for ordinary passports, 90 days/calendar year, effective 24 October 2025. Was diplomatic/service only in conditional_access. Sourced from SATSA (citing DHA Government Notice).

ADDED: Lithuania (LTU) - new visa_free entry for ordinary passports, 90 days/calendar year, effective 24 October 2025. Was diplomatic/official only (30 days) in conditional_access. Sourced from SATSA (citing DHA Government Notice).

ADDED: Slovakia (SVK) - new visa_free entry for ordinary passports, 90 days/calendar year, effective 24 October 2025. Was diplomatic/service only in conditional_access. Sourced from SATSA (citing DHA Government Notice).

CORRECTED: ETA entry updated to reflect that the system launched September/October 2025 (not prospective). Initial phase covered China, India, Indonesia, Mexico; expanding to all visa-required markets. Max stay 90 days (extendable to 180). Entry points OR Tambo and Cape Town initially.
CORRECTED: iso3 for Romania entry was missing; added ROU.
UPDATED: Lesotho entry notes updated to include LEP extension to 28 May 2027 (Minister's Directive No. 21 of 2025).
UPDATED: visa_policy notes and source_urls updated to include DIRCO Sweden ETA notice (September 2025) and SATSA DHA Government Notice source.

UNVERIFIED: Israel, Italy, Jamaica, Japan, Russian Federation remain in visa_free citing the older DIRCO UK page (April 2024) - the Paris PDF (November 2024) does not list them. Their status as ordinary passport holders remains uncertain and is flagged in notes.

2026-06-27 RE-VERIFICATION: Re-fetched DIRCO UK page (dirco.gov.za/uk/visa-countries-waivers/, updated March 2024) and DIRCO Canberra page (dirco.gov.za/canberra/visa-countries/) - both confirm Israel, Italy, Jamaica, Japan, and Russian Federation as ordinary passport visa-free for 90 days. Updated notes on these five entries from 'uncertain' to 'confirmed on two official DIRCO pages, discrepancy with Paris PDF persists'. No new countries added to the exemption list in 2026; DHA March 2026 concession document relates only to processing-delay temporary concessions, not new exemptions. ETA confirmed to have launched (SA News, gov.za news, tourism.gov.za) with initial phase China/India/Indonesia/Mexico; 'fully rolled out' phrase in prior notes consistent with May 2026 update from minister. SATSA-sourced entries for Bulgaria, Croatia, Estonia, Latvia, Lithuania, Romania, Slovakia, Slovenia, Hungary retained - DIRCO Sweden page confirms Estonia/Latvia exemption PDF exists (dirco.gov.za/sweden); underlying Government Notices were not directly readable (binary PDFs). No changes to visa_free list structure, conditional_access, cbi, rbi, or fast_track in this session.

2026-06-29 RE-VERIFICATION: Re-fetched DIRCO UK, DHA exempt-countries page (dha.gov.za/index.php/immigration-services/exempt-countries, issued 9 December 2025), DIRCO Canberra, SATSA, and checked ETA portal. Key findings: (1) DHA official exempt-countries page (Dec 2025) explicitly lists Croatia, Estonia, Slovenia, Bulgaria, Hungary, Lithuania, Romania, Slovakia at 90 days for ordinary passports — confirms all nine East European additions. Source for these seven entries (Bulgaria, Croatia, Hungary, Lithuania, Romania, Slovakia, Slovenia) changed from SATSA (non-official) to DHA official page; source_official updated from false to true. Estonia and Latvia retain DIRCO Sweden official PDF source. (2) DIRCO Canberra and UK pages re-confirm Israel, Italy, Jamaica, Japan, Russian Federation as 90-day ordinary passport visa-free. (3) ETA portal under maintenance at time of fetch but system confirmed operational. (4) No new visa-free countries added since Dec 2025. (5) March 2026 DHA document confirmed as processing-delay concession only, not new exemptions. (6) DHA and DIRCO Canberra pages added to source_urls and sources_checked.
gaps: Specific minimum investment amounts for the Business/Investor Permanent Residence category (Section 27(c)) not published on the official DHA website - Regulation 24(4) details not scraped
Specific minimum net worth or income thresholds for the Financially Independent Permanent Residence category (Section 27(f)) not published on the official DHA page

ETA full nationality eligibility list not explicitly enumerated on eta.dha.gov.za - the complete list of visa-required nationalities eligible for ETA (vs. still requiring a traditional eVisa/visa) has not been officially published in enumerated form
eVisa-eligible country list not explicitly enumerated on the DHA ePermit portal - the FAQ states all non-exempt travelers may apply

Italy, Israel, Jamaica, Japan and Russian Federation: confirmed as visa-free on two official DIRCO pages (UK and Canberra), but remain absent from DIRCO Paris PDF (November 2024). Discrepancy between sources persists - the Paris PDF may simply be an incomplete internal document. Classified as visa_free with notes; downgrade to visa_required would require a clear official disconfirmation not found in June 2026 re-verification.

Government Gazette notice numbers for the August 27 and October 24 2025 ordinary passport exemption additions not retrieved — now confirmed via DHA official exempt-countries page (Dec 2025) which lists all nine countries, but specific Gazette Notice numbers still not retrieved
Processing times for eVisa, ETA, Critical Skills Visa, and Remote Work Visa not explicitly stated on the official pages
Path to citizenship years from permanent residence not explicitly stated on official DHA page
South Africa's Citizenship Act naturalisation requirements (years of PR needed) not verified from official source in this validation session

No credential-based conditional_access rules confirmed: South Africa's visa exemption system is nationality-based. No official DHA or DIRCO source reviewed grants visa-free entry, VoA, eVisa, or ETA based on holding a third-country credential (US visa, Schengen visa, UK visa, residence permit, Green Card, OCI card, or similar).

outbound_official.notes: No official South African government outbound visa-free list for ordinary passport holders exists. DIRCO publishes an outbound visa requirements document exclusively for Diplomatic and Official passport holders (most recent: 'SOUTH AFRICAN DIPLOMATIC AND OFFICIAL PASSPORT HOLDERS VISA REQUIREMENTS', updated 11 June 2025, at dirco.gov.za). The DIRCO 'Advice for South African Citizens Travelling Abroad' page gives generic travel guidance without listing visa-free destinations and explicitly directs citizens to verify requirements with the relevant foreign representative. No DHA or DIRCO page equivalent to India's mea.gov.in/vffin (an outbound ordinary passport visa-free country list) was located. Rely on destination-side data for ordinary passport outbound access.
